title: Vittala Temple and Stone Chariot
text: The Vittala Temple complex at Hampi is associated with the Vijayanagara period. Its ceremonial architecture includes the well-known stone chariot and a large pillared hall.

title: Vijayanagara at Hampi
text: Hampi preserves the remains of the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire. The site contains temples, royal structures, market streets, water systems, and defensive works.

title: Gion Festival
text: Kyoto's Gion Festival includes elaborate yamahoko float processions supported by neighborhood communities. The tradition developed around rituals intended to protect the city from epidemic disease.

title: Kiyomizu-dera
text: Kiyomizu-dera is part of the Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto. The temple is known for a wooden stage projecting from the hillside and for its long religious history.

title: Sankore and Timbuktu Learning
text: Timbuktu became an important center of Islamic learning and trade. Sankore was among the institutions connected with scholars, teaching, and manuscript culture.

title: Timbuktu Manuscript Heritage
text: Timbuktu families and libraries preserved manuscripts on religion, law, astronomy, medicine, and commerce. The collections provide evidence of a substantial written scholarly tradition in West Africa.

title: Historic Sanctuary of Machu Picchu
text: Machu Picchu is an Inca site set between the Peruvian Andes and the Amazon basin. Its terraces, buildings, and engineered landscape show close adaptation to steep mountain terrain.

title: Machu Picchu Water Engineering
text: The settlement used channels, fountains, terraces, and drainage works to manage water. These systems supported occupation while reducing erosion and slope instability.

title: Taj Mahal
text: The Taj Mahal in Agra was commissioned by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan as a mausoleum for Mumtaz Mahal. The complex is celebrated for its white marble architecture, gardens, and symmetrical planning.

title: Agra Fort
text: Agra Fort is a Mughal fortified palace complex built primarily in red sandstone. It served as an imperial residence and contains halls, mosques, and private apartments.

title: Shore Temple
text: The Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram stands near the Bay of Bengal. It belongs to the Pallava-period group of monuments and combines shrines within a coastal stone complex.

title: Descent of the Ganges Relief
text: Mahabalipuram includes a large open-air rock relief often called the Descent of the Ganges or Arjuna's Penance. The carved scene brings together human, divine, and animal figures.
